design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Unequal-fold cross-fitting phase threshold for DML causal estimators. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are Chernozhukov-Chetverikov-Demirer-Duflo-Hansen-Newey-Robins double/debiased ML, standard K-fold cross-fitting implementations, and the AutoID fold-restricted CLT/rate-conversion substrate. Why non-trivial? The target is not ordinary cross-fitting consistency: require a fold-mass spectrum c_k and an explicit phase theorem separating full sqrt(n) asymptotic linearity with variance inflation, vanishing-fold slower-rate regimes, and degenerate leave-one-out limits for orthogonal AIPW/DML scores. Why promising? The nonroutine object is estimator geometry: a hand-derived fold-spectrum certificate and a two-fold triangular-array witness showing the usual DML variance formula fails when one evaluation fold has c_k -> 0. If the delta is only a restatement of sample splitting, path derivatives, or standard CLT bookkeeping, pivot or accept field-tier.

proposed_from.topic: Unequal-fold cross-fitting phase threshold for DML causal estimators. Pre-anchor check: closest anchors are Chernozhukov-Chetverikov-Demirer-Duflo-Hansen-Newey-Robins double/debiased ML, standard K-fold cross-fitting implementations, and the AutoID fold-restricted CLT/rate-conversion substrate. Why non-trivial? The target is not ordinary cross-fitting consistency: require a fold-mass spectrum c_k and an explicit phase theorem separating full sqrt(n) asymptotic linearity with variance inflation, vanishing-fold slower-rate regimes, and degenerate leave-one-out limits for orthogonal AIPW/DML scores. Why promising? The nonroutine object is estimator geometry: a hand-derived fold-spectrum certificate and a two-fold triangular-array witness showing the usual DML variance formula fails when one evaluation fold has c_k -> 0. If the delta is only a restatement of sample splitting, path derivatives, or standard CLT bookkeeping, pivot or accept field-tier.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest CausalSmith entries are downgraded eid_dml_orth_uniform/v1 and failed eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2, whose headline kernels concern orthogonality uniformity, growing-K DML1/DML2 separation, studentization reports, and LRO normalization. The present proposal changes the focal object to a fold-mass-indexed sandwich variance for unbalanced deterministic or repeated cross-fitting folds. No catalogue entry in doc/API.md Section 3 or the adjacent banked DML proposals states this variance object or the two-fold report-separation witness.","published_axis":"ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML and BachEtAl2024DoubleMLR give equal-fold DML inference; AhrensHansenSchafferWiemann2024DDML exposes custom and repeated folds in software; ChiangKatoMaSasaki2022MultiwayDML shows sampling geometry can force a new robust standard error; Velez2024DMLGrowingK studies growing K rather than unequal fold masses. The epsilon gap is the between-fold covariance term indexed by c_k,n: it vanishes in equal fixed folds but survives in the proposed unbalanced triangular-array class."}
seed_list: Construct a fold-spectrum certificate Gamma_n that sharply separates root-n DML, small-fold slower limits, and leave-one-out endpoints for unequal cross-fitting folds.
Give a two-fold triangular-array witness where c_2,n -> 0 invalidates the usual DML score-variance report while the ATE and pointwise nuisance rates stay fixed.
Derive a fold-mass-indexed sandwich variance for deterministic custom DML folds and repeated-fold aggregation.
Characterize a joint allocation frontier linking fold masses, nuisance tuning rates, and orthogonal-score remainders for doubly robust functionals.
Prove a DML1/DML2 equivalence frontier indexed by the fold-mass spectrum rather than by K_n alone.
Classify when unequal-fold DML collapses to the no-splitting stable-estimator regime of Chen-Syrgkanis-Austern.
Construct a fold-balance correction E* that reweights unbalanced evaluation scores to recover the equal-fold influence law when train-fold remainders are homogeneous.
Give a finite-support exact calculation of Gamma_n for binary ATE DML and show the usual product-rate condition is insufficient without fold-mass control.
Develop a repeated-cross-fitting variance decomposition separating observation noise, fold-mass imbalance, and split-randomness components.
State necessary and sufficient fold geometry conditions for the empirical score variance to remain consistent under custom deterministic folds.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a fold-spectrum certificate Gamma_n that sharply separates root-n DML, small-fold slower limits, and leave-one-out endpoints for unequal cross-fitting folds.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, and Robins
anchor_paper.claim: DML uses orthogonal scores and cross-fitting under fixed equal-fold geometry.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: making use of cross-fitting, which provides an efficient form of data-splitting

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=equal folds to triangular unequal folds is stronger because the c_k spectrum creates a phase boundary and leave-one-out endpoint.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML because fold choice is asymptotically immaterial.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their theorem normalizes fixed equal folds; the Stage -1.1 gap notes that Velez-style growing-K behavior does not collapse to equal-fold asymptotics.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML because Gamma_n is an observed fold-geometry object with three regimes, not a fixed-K product-rate condition.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine CLT bookkeeping is promoted to a new certificate, phase frontier, and small-fold witness class.

adversarial_referee.instance: May read as standard triangular-array CLT bookkeeping.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Gamma_n is exhibited in Section 9 and indexes estimator validity, variance, and endpoint regimes.

adversarial_referee.instance: Velez may already cover growing K.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Velez compares DML1/DML2 under K growth, not unequal evaluation-fold mass with c_min phases.

adversarial_referee.instance: Two-fold witness could be hand tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: Section 8 states an open class and Section 9 perturbs a nonzero variance component.

seed_details.burned_reason: D-0.5 reviews found the fold-spectrum certificate promissory, the vanishing-fold variance witness mathematically wrong, and the fold-mass sandwich pivot not-publishable.

seed_details.one_line: Give a two-fold triangular-array witness where c_2,n -> 0 invalidates the usual DML score-variance report while the ATE and pointwise nuisance rates stay fixed.

anchor_paper.author: Ahrens, Hansen, Schaffer, and Wiemann
anchor_paper.claim: ddml supports custom fold variables and repeated cross-fitting while reporting conventional inference.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: foldvar( varlist ) is the integer variable to specify custom folds

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample is stronger if promoted from one small fold to an open two-fold triangular-array class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a bad user-chosen split, not a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The software exposes custom folds; the witness identifies exactly when the usual variance report fails asymptotically.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not AhrensHansenSchafferWiemann2024DDML because it targets asymptotic variance validity under custom fold mass, not implementation options.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the witness produces a non-normalization variance gap V0+omega^2 versus V0.

adversarial_referee.instance: Small fold may contain finitely many observations.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: that is the boundary; the conjecture demands an open class around lambda in (0,infty).

adversarial_referee.instance: ddml may not claim validity for arbitrary custom folds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the paper is a practice-facing consumer, not a theorem comparator for arbitrary validity.
Rebuttal: Exhibit 9.1 computes Gamma_n and Exhibit 9.2 computes the variance gap.

seed_details.burned_reason: D-0.5 reviews found the fold-spectrum certificate promissory, the vanishing-fold variance witness mathematically wrong, and the fold-mass sandwich pivot not-publishable.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a fold-mass-indexed sandwich variance for deterministic custom DML folds and repeated-fold aggregation.

anchor_paper.author: Chiang, Kato, Ma, and Sasaki
anchor_paper.claim: Changing DML sampling geometry can require a new cross-fitting algorithm and variance formula.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Changing the DML sampling geometry to multiway clusters required a new cross-fitting algorithm

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends geometry but stronger because it creates a fold-mass variance component, not a new cluster case.
seed_details.referee_objection: Cluster-robust DML already teaches that geometry changes variance.
seed_details.rebuttal: Cluster geometry is dependence-driven; this seed isolates evaluation-fold mass under iid observations.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ChiangKatoMaSasaki2022MultiwayDML because the new object is c_k-indexed split geometry under iid sampling.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: variance formula would include fold-mass and split-randomness components not present in ordinary score variance.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could apply to many DML scores.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: start with ATE AIPW then state score-class extension only as scope.
lands; downgrade to field-tier if pursued alone.

adversarial_referee.instance: Multiway DML might subsume design changes broadly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: it handles clustered dependence, not deterministic fold-mass imbalance.

seed_details.burned_reason: D-0.5 reviews found the fold-spectrum certificate promissory, the vanishing-fold variance witness mathematically wrong, and the fold-mass sandwich pivot not-publishable.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize a joint allocation frontier linking fold masses, nuisance tuning rates, and orthogonal-score remainders for doubly robust functionals.

anchor_paper.author: McGrath and Mukherjee
anchor_paper.claim: Nuisance tuning choices and sample-splitting strategies jointly determine rates for doubly robust functionals.
anchor_paper.location: Gaps payload source_refs claim; arXiv https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.14857
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: nuisance tuning choices and sample-splitting strategies jointly determine optimal rates

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens product-rate conditions but stronger because allocation and nuisance tuning are jointly optimized.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just ChernozhukovNeweySingh2023FiniteSampleDML with c_k inserted.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is the optimization frontier over fold mass and nuisance tuning, not rate conversion after a split is fixed.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ChernozhukovNeweySingh2023FiniteSampleDML because fold allocation is the endogenous frontier variable.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if it yields inequalities over c_k and rates with distinct regimes; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: would need boundary witnesses for necessity; otherwise field-tier.
Rebuttal: use Gamma_n inequalities as the focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims M3 but no critical delta.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: critical object is allocation frontier, not delta; label M9 if needed.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a DML1/DML2 equivalence frontier indexed by the fold-mass spectrum rather than by K_n alone.

anchor_paper.author: Velez
anchor_paper.claim: Growing K separates DML1 and DML2 in bias and MSE.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: DML2 asymptotically dominates DML1 in terms of bias and mean squared error

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Equivalence frontier stronger than K-growth if c_k spectrum changes the sign or magnitude of DML1/DML2 separation.
seed_details.referee_objection: Velez already studies the relevant asymptotics.
seed_details.rebuttal: Velez indexes by growing K, while the seed uses unequal masses at fixed or triangular K.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Velez2024DMLGrowingK because c_k distribution, not K alone, is the frontier coordinate.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if it yields a second-comparator frontier; weaker than chosen seed if only DML1/DML2.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only DML1 and DML2 are compared.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: lands; add equal-fold DML and no-splitting endpoint or downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: Latest Velez version may cover more regimes.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal requires checking latest version before pursuit.
Rebuttal: M2 needs threshold, not correction, but threshold must be explicit.

seed_details.one_line: Classify when unequal-fold DML collapses to the no-splitting stable-estimator regime of Chen-Syrgkanis-Austern.

anchor_paper.author: Chen, Syrgkanis, and Austern
anchor_paper.claim: No-splitting DML is valid under leave-one-out stability.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: when these auxiliary estimation algorithms satisfy natural leave-one-out stability properties

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary shift is stronger because c_min determines when cross-fitting becomes no-splitting-like.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is only a restatement of leave-one-out stability.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed theorem identifies when fold geometry forces that endpoint, rather than assuming no splitting from the start.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ChenSyrgkanisAustern2022NoSplitDML because it derives the endpoint as c_min -> 1/n from cross-fitting geometry.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the boundary n c_min is necessary; otherwise field-tier.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: chosen draft labels it as a conjectural phase boundary.
Rebuttal: state only 'can hold only under' in the endpoint class.

adversarial_referee.instance: No-splitting paper may cover some cross-fit extremes.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: it does not index deterministic fold masses.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a fold-balance correction E* that reweights unbalanced evaluation scores to recover the equal-fold influence law when train-fold remainders are homogeneous.

anchor_paper.author: Bach, Chernozhukov, Kurz, Spindler, and Klaassen
anchor_paper.claim: DoubleML implements K-fold DML with score-based inference.
anchor_paper.location: Gaps payload source_refs claim; DOI https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v108.i03
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: DML1/DML2 with K-fold partitions whose folds have size n/K

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Decompose-then-correct is stronger if E* changes inference, weaker if it is only inverse fold weighting.
seed_details.referee_objection: Reweighting by fold size is algebraically obvious.
seed_details.rebuttal: Likely true unless nuisance-training remainder terms enter; keep as field-tier fallback.

seed_details.one_line: Give a finite-support exact calculation of Gamma_n for binary ATE DML and show the usual product-rate condition is insufficient without fold-mass control.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh
anchor_paper.claim: Finite-sample DML translates learner rates into inference conditions without making fold allocation the object.
anchor_paper.location: Gaps payload source_refs claim; DOI https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/asac033
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: translates learner rates into inference conditions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Finite-support calculation is useful but may be too special unless tied to a generic open class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a toy example of the rate theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Use it as Exhibit 9.1 supporting the flagship phase theorem, not as standalone flagship.

seed_details.one_line: Develop a repeated-cross-fitting variance decomposition separating observation noise, fold-mass imbalance, and split-randomness components.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, and Robins
anchor_paper.claim: DML treats split randomness as finite-sample relevant and recommends repeated splitting.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Variance decomposition stronger if asymptotic split-randomness survives under unbalanced masses.
seed_details.referee_objection: Repeated splitting is already a simulation-stabilization device.
seed_details.rebuttal: This seed asks when it becomes an asymptotic variance component, but needs a sharper witness.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless a closed-form split variance is derived

seed_details.one_line: State necessary and sufficient fold geometry conditions for the empirical score variance to remain consistent under custom deterministic folds.

anchor_paper.author: Ahrens, Hansen, Schaffer, and Wiemann
anchor_paper.claim: ddml exposes custom folds and fold-frequency tables.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: tabfold prints a table with frequency of observations by fold

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Necessary-and-sufficient claim is stronger than software warning but may be narrow.
seed_details.referee_objection: A variance consistency condition is not a publishable causal theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: As standalone likely field-tier; as part of Gamma_n phase theorem it is a useful component.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 gaps subsumed the broad scan. ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML anchors equal fixed K-fold DML asymptotic linearity; BachEtAl2024DoubleMLR and AhrensHansenSchafferWiemann2024DDML show the software-facing equal/custom/repeated-fold practice; Velez2024DMLGrowingK opens the growing-K flank by separating DML1/DML2; ChenSyrgkanisAustern2022NoSplitDML gives the no-splitting endpoint through leave-one-out stability; NeweyRobins2018CrossFittingFastRemainders and McGrathMukherjee2022NuisanceTuningSplitting motivate split-dependent remainder rates; ChernozhukovNeweySingh2023FiniteSampleDML translates nuisance rates into inference but does not make fold allocation the object; ChiangKatoMaSasaki2022MultiwayDML shows sampling geometry can change DML variance formulas. The retained gap is a fold-mass phase theorem with a computable certificate and a two-fold triangular-array witness.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","mode":"pivot","version":1,"message":"Fold-mass sandwich variance for unbalanced cross-fitted AIPW/DML estimators, with a two-fold report-separation witness.","literature_map":"Chernozhukov et al. (2017, AER P&P, ChernozhukovEtAl2017DMLATE) provide the ATE-facing DML anchor. Bach et al. (2024, JSS, BachEtAl2024DoubleMLR) implement DoubleML with standard K-fold score-based inference. Ahrens et al. (2024, Stata Journal, AhrensHansenSchafferWiemann2024DDML) expose custom fold variables and repeated cross-fitting in software practice. Velez (2024, arXiv/latest 2026, Velez2024DMLGrowingK) opens the growing-K flank by separating DML1 and DML2. Newey and Robins (2018, arXiv, NeweyRobins2018CrossFitting) show split construction can affect semiparametric remainder behavior. McGrath and Mukherjee (2022, arXiv, McGrathMukherjee2022NuisanceTuningSplitting) link nuisance tuning and sample-splitting strategy for doubly robust functionals. Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2023, Biometrika, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2023FiniteSampleDML) translate learner rates into finite-sample DML inference conditions. Chiang et al. (2022, JBES, ChiangKatoMaSasaki2022MultiwayDML) show a changed DML sampling geometry can require a new robust standard-error formula. Chen, Syrgkanis, and Austern (2022, arXiv, ChenSyrgkanisAustern2022NoSplitDML) give the no-splitting endpoint under leave-one-out stability. banked_reason: Stopped early after D-0.5 rejected the initial fold-spectrum phase angle as field-tier and rejected the fold-mass sandwich pivot as not-publishable; the DML unequal-fold topic collapsed into standard triangular-array/variance bookkeeping with broken witness claims.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Construct a fold-spectrum certificate Gamma_n that sharply separates root-n DML, small-fold slower limits, and leave-one-out endpoints for unequal cross-fitting folds.
seeds_burned.reason: D-0.5 reviews found the fold-spectrum certificate promissory, the vanishing-fold variance witness mathematically wrong, and the fold-mass sandwich pivot not-publishable.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Give a two-fold triangular-array witness where c_2,n -> 0 invalidates the usual DML score-variance report while the ATE and pointwise nuisance rates stay fixed.
seeds_burned.reason: D-0.5 reviews found the fold-spectrum certificate promissory, the vanishing-fold variance witness mathematically wrong, and the fold-mass sandwich pivot not-publishable.
